content.full_text:
Farn Polrrrcal PRAcrrcns C oMMrssroN
P.O. Box 807 c 428 J Street r Sacramento, CA 95812-0g07
(916)322-5660 o Fax (916) 322-0886
June 26,2002
Marcia H. Armstrong
9216 Smokey Lane
Ft Jones, CA 96032
Re: Your Request for Informal Assistance
Our File No. I-02-165
Dear Ms. Armstrong:
This is in response to your request for assistance under the Political Reform Act
(the "Act").' Becauie your letter does not seek advice regarding any provision of the
Act, we are treating your request as one for informal assistance.2
QUESTION AND CONCLUSION
You were elected to the Siskiyou County Board of Supervisors in March ZOO2.
Your term of office does not begin until January 2003. Are there any restrictions on your
attendance as "supervisor-elect" at social, community and civic er"nts before your term
ofoffice begins in January of2003?
The Fair Political Practices Commission interprets and enforces the provisions of
the Political Reform Act, which regulates such things as disclosure of campaign
contributions and expenditures during elections, conflict-of-interest issues facing public
officials and financing of lobbying activities before the state Legislature and staie
administrative agencies. The question you pose is not under thejurisdiction of the Fair
Political Practices Commission. It is suggested you contact youi county counsel for
assistance with this issue.
rGovernmentCode$$81000-91014. CommissionregulationsappearatTitle2, $$lgt09-Lgg97,
of the California Code of Regulations.
2lnformal assistancJdoes not provide the requestor with the immunity provided by an opinion or
formal written advice (Government Code g 83114; 2 Cal. Code of Regs. $ f SjZg(c)(:), .opy enclosed.)


File No. LA2-165
Page2
You do need to be aware, however, that as an elected officer of a local
governmental agency, you may not receive gifts aggregating more than $320 in value in a
calendar year from a single source. Additionally, when you do assume office in January,
you will disclose on your statement of economic interests any gifts valued at $50 or more
received in the prior 12 months from a single source. Gifts include such things as meals
or tickets to sooial or sports venues. I have enclosed a fact sheet on the receipt of gifts,
travel and honoraria to assist you further.
If you have any questions please feel free to contact me at (866) 275-3772
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
General Counsel
UI.,J:(
6cn
By: Jeanette
Political
Technical Assistance Division
Enclosures
